items.title: Memory layout and indexing
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  subgraph Indices
    direction LR
    I0["0"]
    I1["1"]
    I2["2"]
    I3["3"]
    I4["4"]
  end
  subgraph Memory["Contiguous memory (random access in O(1))"]
    direction LR
    C0["a"] --- C1["b"] --- C2["c"] --- C3["d"] --- C4["e"]
  end
  I0 --> C0
  I1 --> C1
  I2 --> C2
  I3 --> C3
  I4 --> C4
items.caption: Items live in contiguous memory; the index is converted to an address by simple arithmetic, so lst[i] is O(1).

items.title: insert(0, x) shifts everything
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  subgraph Before
    direction LR
    B0["a"] --- B1["b"] --- B2["c"] --- B3["d"]
  end
  subgraph After["insert(0, 'X') — every later item moved one slot right"]
    direction LR
    A0["X"] --- A1["a"] --- A2["b"] --- A3["c"] --- A4["d"]
  end
  Before --> After
items.caption: Insert/pop at the front is O(n) because each later element shifts. Use collections.deque if you need both ends to be cheap.
